refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR includes multiple obligations: analyzing terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and clarity, it is necessary to split these into separate sub-FRs, each focusing on a single aspect of worksite area analysis. This avoids conflating multiple requirements and maintains solution neutrality by focusing on what the system must do, not how.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and provide a representation of the terrain within the worksite area, independent of obstacle or work-zone data.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including obstacle representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including work-zone representation.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing the physical features of the worksite
Create a semantic map annotating the terrain model with categorized site features relevant to planning and safety
Integrate terrain and feature data into a unified worksite model accessible to planning and execution modules
Provide an interface layer that abstracts the worksite model for consumption by other system functions
selected_dp.selected_dp: Create a semantic map annotating the terrain model with categorized site features relevant to planning and safety

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically separates terrain representation from semantic annotation, supporting modular updates and independent evolution of terrain data and feature categorization. This reduces coupling between raw data acquisition and higher-level planning functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes that terrain data can be abstracted sufficiently to support semantic annotation without tight coupling to specific sensor types or data formats
Risk that semantic categories may evolve requiring updates to the DP or its interfaces
Assumes that planning and execution functions can consume the semantic map without requiring direct access to raw terrain data
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the boundary between terrain representation and semantic annotation to ensure modularity and support independent updates; consider explicitly defining interfaces for worksite model access to reduce integration risks.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for processing terrain data and generating semantic maps with categorized site features for planning and safety

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives terrain model data and sensor inputs for semantic annotation

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands to initiate semantic mapping tasks

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts related to identified safety-relevant site features in the semantic map

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as semantic mapping is core to NGHE processing; minimal diffusion as outputs are alerts and annotated maps.
Low containment; vehicle mainly executes commands and moves, not semantic processing.
Very low containment; teleoperation focuses on remote control, not terrain annotation.
Moderate containment; safety services use semantic info but do not create it, some diffusion expected.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing the semantic mapping DP because it directly processes terrain data and generates annotated maps, maintaining high cohesion and low diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Safety Services if safety-related annotations require close integration, risking diffusion of semantic mapping logic.
Dependency on accurate telemetry data may cause coupling with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If diffusion with Safety Services is problematic, consider refining the DP to separate pure semantic annotation from safety-specific feature extraction, allowing clearer module boundaries.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by specifying creation of a semantic map that annotates the terrain model with categorized site features relevant to planning and safety, fulfilling the requirement for worksite area analysis including terrain representation.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Safety Services if safety-related annotations require close integration, risking diffusion of semantic mapping logic.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and containment for the semantic mapping DP with minimal diffusion, as indicated by the high containment score (9) and low diffusion score (2). Other modules have low containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module receives terrain data and commands, processes semantic mapping, and outputs alerts and annotated maps, which can be validated against acceptance criteria requiring availability of a worksite model/map with relevant site features.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibilities to the NGHE Solution module with well-defined external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert) supports traceability from FR through DP to IM.

recommended_revisions: Consider refining the DP to separate pure semantic annotation from safety-specific feature extraction to reduce diffusion risk with Safety Services and clarify module boundaries.

decision.justification: Although all gates passed indicating the current FR, DP, and IM are aligned and feasible, the recommended revision suggests refining the DP to separate pure semantic annotation from safety-specific feature extraction. This refinement is important to reduce diffusion risk with the Safety Services module and clarify module boundaries, which is critical given the high safety criticality of the system. Addressing this now will improve architectural clarity and reduce integration risks before proceeding to next-level sub-FRs.

revise.why: To separate semantic annotation from safety-specific feature extraction, reducing diffusion risk and clarifying module boundaries with Safety Services.

assumptions: The current DP combines semantic mapping and safety-related feature extraction.
High safety criticality demands clear module boundaries to minimize diffusion risks.
open_risks: Potential overlap and diffusion of semantic mapping logic with Safety Services if not separated.
Risk of unclear allocation of safety-related functions leading to integration issues.
evidence_needed: Detailed analysis of semantic annotation versus safety feature extraction functions.
Impact assessment of DP refinement on module cohesion and diffusion scores.
